mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations. A sustained month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days through lower cost of capital.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.51% as of t-1mo suggests monetary accommodation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering equity risk-off behavior. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice recession risk.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth and retail sales growth jointly signal robust aggregate demand. When both PAYEMS growth and RSAFS growth are positive month-over-month, equity investors increase risk appetite, producing positive 20-day forward returns. The interaction captures demand momentum that neither series alone fully represents.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 148230.0k and RSAFS at 492008.0M as of t-2mo
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained demand growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day returns in high-demand regimes are not significantly higher than low-demand regimes (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index captures real economic capacity utilization. When INDPRO declines month-over-month, it signals supply-side weakness or demand destruction, causing equity investors to reduce growth expectations and produce negative 60-day forward returns as earnings revisions cascade downward.
regime_hint: supply-side contraction or demand-driven production slowdown
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite declining industrial production, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and real GDP growth (GDPC1) jointly capture the strength of the demand cycle. When HOUST is above its 12-month median AND GDPC1 growth is positive, the economy is in a robust expansion phase that supports equity valuations. This interaction identifies genuine demand-side momentum distinct from noise in either series alone.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1236.0k and GDPC1 at 17286.497B as of t-2mo and t-6mo respectively
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion with housing strength
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns in high-demand regimes are not significantly higher than low-demand regimes (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL above 3% combined with FEDFUNDS below 2% signals real negative rates that erode bond returns and redirect capital to equities as inflation hedge. This regime-specific mechanism activates only when real rates are deeply negative, producing positive 20-day returns through portfolio reallocation.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 249.462 (YoY ~3.8%) and FEDFUNDS at 1.51% as of t-1mo
regime_hint: negative real rates with inflation above target
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative real rates, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window in the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth above 150k per month signals sustained labor market health and consumer income growth. This always-on trend signal produces positive 5-day forward returns as strong employment data supports equity valuations through higher expected consumer spending and corporate earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 148230.0k as of t-1mo indicates near-threshold employment strength
regime_hint: any regime with positive labor market momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in months with PAYEMS > 150k are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline of more than 1% month-over-month signals consumer demand destruction and signals early-cycle weakness. This threshold-triggered mechanism produces negative 20-day forward returns as investors reprice earnings expectations downward in response to visible consumer spending deterioration.
regime_hint: demand-side contraction or consumer spending weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite retail sales declines, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
